summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn concluded the 'Match Made in Heaven' series with a message titled 'There's Always Hope,' anchored in 1 Corinthians 13:7's declaration that 'love always hopes.' He began by reading 1 Corinthians 13 and Romans 15:13, emphasizing that God is the 'God of hope' who fills believers with joy and peace. Pastor Travis defined biblical hope as confident expectation, not just wishful thinking, and contrasted the world's fleeting joy and peace with the unfailing, supernatural hope found in God. He offered four points of encouragement for those feeling hopeless: first, to remember that God is always on our side and near to the brokenhearted; second, that God sees every detail of our situation and cares deeply; third, that God's timing is perfect, and waiting cultivates patience and trust; and fourth, that in our weakness, God's strength is made perfect. Using personal stories and practical illustrations—like trying to fix a broken wall with broken tools—he warned against trying to solve spiritual brokenness with worldly, broken solutions like substance abuse or unhealthy relationships. He urged the congregation to turn to God as their only true resource, to step out in faith to see the supernatural, and to allow God to develop their character through life's trials. The message closed with a call to surrender to Christ and a reminder that God's power is most evident when our own strength ends.
mainPoints: Remember that God is on your side; He is near to the brokenhearted and is always with you.
God sees your situation completely and cares about your anguish with unfailing love.
In your weakness, God's strength is made perfect; His power works when our human power ends.

versesMentioned.text: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
Now faith is confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we do not see.
The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
I will praise you forever for what you have done; in your name I will hope, for your name is good.
So do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you and help you; I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.

Record my misery; list my tears on your scroll— are they not in your record? Then my enemies will turn back when I call for help. By this I will know that God is for me. In God, whose word I praise, in the Lord, whose word I praise— in God I trust and am not afraid. What can man do to me?
I will be glad and rejoice in your love, for you saw my affliction and knew the anguish of my soul.

"For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways," declares the Lord. "As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts."

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.
Against all hope, Abraham in hope believed and so became the father of many nations, just as it had been said to him, "So shall your offspring be."

But he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness." Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ's power may rest on me. That is why, for Christ's sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 34:18 (NIV) - 'The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.'
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel broken or discouraged. Write it down and consciously remind yourself that God is with you in it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being near to me. Help me to feel your presence in my situation today and trust that you are working.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 31:7 (NIV) - 'I will be glad and rejoice in your love, for you saw my affliction and knew the anguish of my soul.'
Tuesday.action: Take a moment of quiet reflection. Verbally or in writing, tell God about a specific worry, trusting that He sees and cares.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I am grateful that you see every detail of my life. I release my anguish to you, believing in your unfailing love.

Wednesday.scripture: Isaiah 55:8-9 (NIV) - 'For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways...'
Wednesday.action: Choose one thing you are impatiently waiting for. Practice patience by not trying to force a solution today.
Wednesday.prayer: God, help me to trust that your timing is perfect. Teach me patience and help me to wait on you with hope.

Thursday.scripture: James 1:2-4 (NIV) - 'Consider it pure joy... whenever you face trials...'
Thursday.action: Identify a current challenge. Instead of asking for it to be removed, ask God what He wants to develop in you through it.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, give me your perspective on my trials. Help me to find joy in the spiritual maturity you are building in me.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 (NIV) - 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.'
Friday.action: Acknowledge one personal weakness or limitation. Decide to rely on God's strength in that area instead of your own.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I admit my weakness. I choose to rely on your strength today. Let your power be perfected in me.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 15:13 (NIV) - 'May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on the past week. Write down one reason you have for hope because of God's faithfulness.
Saturday.prayer: God of hope, fill me with your joy and peace. Let my life overflow with hope so that I can encourage others.
